payload.title: VS Code extension wrapping trust-lsp
payload.description:
Wrap the existing trust-lsp server (RT-36) in a VS Code extension so install is one click.

Tasks:
- New directory: editors/vscode/ (out-of-tree from the Rust workspace).
- TypeScript LSP client using vscode-languageclient. Boilerplate is short — see rust-analyzer's extension as a reference but don't copy its scale.
- Binary distribution: ship platform binaries for darwin-arm64, darwin-x64, linux-x64, linux-arm64, win-x64 in the .vsix. Alternative: download on first activation. Vendored is heavier but more reliable for the agent audience.
- Activation: on .rs files in a workspace that has trust-attrs in deps OR a #![strict] line OR a .trust marker file. Don't activate for non-Trust Rust projects.
- Marketplace metadata: name 'Trust', description, icon (skip if no time), keywords. Publish to both VS Code Marketplace and Open VSX.
- Minimal feature set: diagnostics + hover + go-to-def (whatever trust-lsp already serves). No quick-fixes / refactorings — defer.

Done when: Open VS Code -> Install 'Trust' -> open a strict Rust file -> see R-rule diagnostics inline. Screenshot in extension README.

Not blocked by RT-58 (publishing crates), but better if the trust-lsp binary downloaded by the extension matches a published version. Worth coordinating timing.
